notes: Master-Reorg Ship B2 (router gating, part 2 of 4) — delete the two dead ops-tool functions + clean the nav_restore branches. Redesign-only, legacy byte-identical; FIRST nav_restore edit of Ship B. WHAT: (1) nav_restore 'tool:' branch repointed from deploy_showOpsTool to rd_openOpsTool — BOTH the `typeof` condition AND the call changed in lockstep (the adversarial-verify catch: changing only the call would leave `typeof deploy_showOpsTool === 'function'` false after the delete → the branch silently skipped → Work-tool back-nav restore blanks the host; a No-Silent-Failure violation). This branch is LIVE: rd_openOpsTool pushes d:'tool:'+tab for the Work ops cards (BOM/Manifest/Port Map/Rack Map), so the repoint is done in the SAME edit that drops 'tools' — no window where it references a deleted fn. (2) nav_restore 'tools' branch removed. (3) deploy_showTools + deploy_showOpsTool functions DELETED — already dead code: their only live door (the ⚙ TOOLS button) became a direct ⚑ BLOCKER button in R-2b (.171) and no redesign door has fed them since. KEPT: DEPLOY_TOOLS registry + _deploy_toolsBackHdr (shared by the surviving rd_openOpsTool + deploy_detailTool — deleting them would break the live Work-card + Deploy-detail tool renders). STRANDING: none — the grid's tenants all have live redesign doors (bom/manifest/portmap/rackmap → rd_openOpsTool Work cards; burndown/audits/blast → deploy_detailTool Deploy-detail buttons; Log-a-Blocker → blocker_quick ⚑). Honest note: the 'sops' ops-tool still renders into the inert hidden #ops-content under rd — a pre-existing deferred R-2b/R4 gap, NOT introduced or worsened here. Also corrected 5 now-stale comments that described these functions as 'kept as shims'/live (comment-rot honesty). LEGACY: neither function had a legacy door and ?legacy=1 never pushes 'tools'/'tool:' (rd_openOpsTool early-returns under legacy; legacy ops uses showOpsTab/#ops-tab-strip), so both removed branches are unreachable under the rip-cord → byte-identical. Worst case: a pre-Ship-B session snapshot carrying d:'tools' no longer restores the retired grid (typeof-guard already false) — user lands on the restored page, no crash, no legacy resurrection. No new assets; CSS unchanged (braces 3560); 3-stamp lockstep; gates green (4 blocks compile, chain valid, CRLF preserved). DEVICE-VERIFY (HARD-STOP, clear SW cache first): under the DEFAULT (redesign) URL — (1) Work→Deploy shows the ⚑ BLOCKER button (not ⚙ TOOLS); (2) the four Work tool cards (BOM/Manifest/Port Map/Rack Map) each open and render; (3) Deploy-detail BURNDOWN/AUDITS/BLAST buttons open; (4) Log-a-Blocker one-taps; (5) open a Work tool card, navigate away, then use browser/gesture Back to force a restore — the tool re-renders into the Work host with the correct Back-to-grid header (this exercises the repointed 'tool:' branch). ?legacy=1: the legacy #ops-tab-strip tools still work, byte-identical. ROLLBACK: revert the commit.
